item.title: Design system tokens and color palette
item.intent: The design system must establish a dark-primary palette with very dark blue-black backgrounds (#0a0a0f to #111118 range), light text, and amber/warm gold (#d4a853 range) as the single surgical accent color. The palette signals craftsmanship and maturity — forged, not vibed. Replace the current primary orange (#b86b3a) with a refined amber/gold scale. Define surface hierarchy (background, card, elevated), text hierarchy (primary, secondary, muted), and accent usage rules in Tailwind v4 @theme tokens. Note: FEAT-002 already defined --font-prose, --font-mono, and --color-primary in @theme. This sprint extends those tokens with surface/text hierarchy and replaces the orange scale with amber/gold. Update .prose class colors to use the new tokens where hardcoded hex values exist.
steps: Define dark surface colors in @theme: background (#0a0a0f–#111118), card (slightly elevated), elevated (modals/popovers)
Define text color hierarchy: primary (off-white), secondary (muted light), muted (dim)
Replace primary orange scale with amber/gold accent scale centered on #d4a853
Define accent usage rules: links, CTAs, active states, code syntax highlights — sparingly
Define border/divider treatments for dark surfaces (subtle, low-contrast)
Document token decisions in CSS comments

acceptanceCriteria.text: Tailwind theme defines dark surface tokens, text hierarchy, and amber/gold accent scale
